acceptance.assertion: A reproducible benchmark records raw and compressed bytes, duplicate identity payload count, DOM node cost, JavaScript parse cost and representative low-power-device load behavior for the current catalogue.
A documented and tested threshold states when the current bootstrap-plus-static-projection design must change, without treating catalogue count alone as a performance proxy.

Generated bootstrap, HTML hydration, segmented static JSON and demand-loaded read-only static delivery are compared for accessibility, SEO, no-JavaScript behavior, privacy, caching, complexity and Pages compatibility.
Every public representation continues to derive from the canonical CommonProject files and no second mutable catalogue truth is introduced.
The selected implementation measurably reduces avoidable transfer or parsing work and has regression tests that fail when the agreed delivery budget is exceeded.
Closeout includes the complete reviewed diff, exact-head tests, browser performance evidence, green CI, Pages deployment and public readback on representative mobile and desktop paths.

approval.note: Measure first. Do not introduce a backend, runtime API or HTML-as-source design without comparative evidence. Preserve the canonical catalog, text/no-JavaScript access, Pages deployment and privacy boundaries.

goal: Measure the actual transfer, parse and DOM cost of Commonworld's generated JavaScript bootstrap and repeated static catalogue projections, define a repository-enforced scaling threshold, and implement the smallest static delivery design that preserves one canonical CommonProject truth, no-JavaScript access, SEO, privacy and GitHub Pages operation.

does_not_establish: that HTML hydration is the preferred design
that a backend or runtime API is justified
that 41 entries already violate a user-visible budget
permission to weaken no-JavaScript or privacy behavior
metadata.observed_limit: At 41 entries the same canonical records are delivered through a generated JavaScript bootstrap and repeated static card projections. This is functional, but the next design decision must be driven by measured transfer and parse cost rather than intuition.

title: Measure and reduce duplicate catalogue delivery
